title: ononis arvensis var. antiquorum
intro: Ononis arvensis var. antiquorum, also known as the "Burnet" or "Field Burnet", is a flowering plant in the legume family that is native to Europe and parts of Asia. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it useful for treating wounds and skin irritations. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever and alleviating symptoms of respiratory infections, and has been used in herbal preparations to create teas, infusions, and tinctures to support overall well-being. In herbal medicine, the roots and leaves of Ononis arvensis var. antiquorum are typically used, and it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
